Off Calendar Mar-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF GEOFFREY SHMIGELSKY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT

Appearances:
Samuel Song, esq. (415-766-7451) of Gaw Poe LLP for Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky.
Nicole S. Healy, esq. (650-780-1733) of RMKB Lawyers for Cross-complainant GetAround.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Geoffrey Shmigelsky's demurrers to the numbered causes of action in Getaround, Inc.'s first amended cross-complaint (FACC) are ruled upon as follows:

1 (Breach of Contract). "Where a complaint is based on a written contract which it sets out in full, a general demurrer to the complaint admits not only the contents of the instrument but also any pleaded meaning to which the instrument is reasonably susceptible." (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) So here. As to Civil Code 1668, the stock transfer agreements are reasonably susceptible to the non-fraudulent meanings pled. This demurrer is overruled.

2 (Promissory Estoppel). Causes of action may be pled in the alternative; Shmigelsky's other arguments are the same as for breach of contract. This demurrer is overruled.

3 (Fraud in the Inducement). The FACC's fraud cause of action fails to plead particularized facts showing that any promise was false - much less knowingly false - when made. (See Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30-31.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

4 (Violation of Corporations Code 25401). A civil suit for a 25401 violation is limited to a person who "purchases a security" from the violator. (Corp. Code 25501; Apollo Capital Fund LLC v. Roth Capital Partners, LLC (2007) 158 Cal.App.4th 226, 252-53.) This demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. SAMUEL SONG, ESQ. of Gaw Poe LLP (Tel: 415-766-7451) appearing for plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky;
Atty. JAMES N. KRAMER, ESQ. of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (Tel: 415-773-5923) appearing for defendants Mohamed Zaid and Ilan Kroo;
Atty. EDWIN B. BARNES, ESQ. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley (Tel: 650-364-8200) appearing for defendants Getaround, Inc., Sam Zaid, and Adam Kosmicki.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MOHAMED ZAID, ILAN KROO DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Ilan Kroo and Mohamed Zaid's demurrer to Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to the third, fourth, and eighth causes of action for aiding and abetting fraud, civil conspiracy to defraud, and unjust enrichment and sustained with leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for insider trading.

The third and fourth causes of action fail in light of the Court's ruling on the Getaround Defendants' demurrer dismissing the underlying fraud claims. Without a primary fraud claim, secondary causes of action such as aiding and abetting and conspiracy also fail. (See Richard B. LeVine, Inc. v. Higashi (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 566, 575-576 [claims for aiding and abetting and conspiracy fail if the underlying tort fails because both claims seek to vindicate the same primary right].) The eighth cause of action for unjust enrichment fails because such a claim does not lie where express binding agreements, such as the Stock Transfer Agreements, exist to define the rights of the parties. (Barnes Decl. Exs. A-E; see California Medical Assn., Inc. v. Aetna U.S. Healthcare of California, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 151, 172 ["[A]s a matter of law, a quasi-contract action for unjust enrichment does not lie where, as here, express binding agreements exist and define the parties' rights."].)

The sixth cause of action for insider trading is pled with insufficient specificity. (FAC 70, 117 [allegations on information and belief that Purchaser Defendants committed insider trading based on purchase of stock and close familial and personal relationships with Getaround officers]; see In re VeriSign, Inc. Derivative Litigation (N.D. Cal. 2007) 531 F.Supp.2d 1173, 1221 [section 25402 claims should be pled with particularity "as insider trading under California law is a fraudulent practice"]; Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc. (2003) 30 Cal.4th 167, 184 ["In California, fraud must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice."] [citation omitted].) Generally alleging that Purchaser Defendants likely had access to information concealed from Plaintiff does not satisfy the particularity standard for this cause of action. Plaintiff has 30 days leave to amend to add specific factual allegations regarding the information allegedly shared by Getaround Defendants and obtained by each of the Purchaser Defendants. Furthermore, Plaintiff should allege if he can do so in good faith that Getaround has a class of equity security held of record by 500 or more persons as required to qualify for a remedy under Corporations Code section 25502.5.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

APPEARANCES: Atty. SAMUEL SONG, ESQ. of Gaw Poe LLP (Tel: 415-766-7451) appearing for plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky;
Atty. JAMES N. KRAMER, ESQ. of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP (Tel: 415-773-5923) appearing for defendants Mohamed Zaid and Ilan Kroo;
Atty. EDWIN B. BARNES, ESQ. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley (Tel: 650-364-8200) appearing for defendants Getaround, Inc., Sam Zaid, and Adam Kosmicki.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GETAROUND, INC., SAM ZAID, AND ADAM KOSMICKI'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Getaround, Inc., Sam Zaid, and Adam Kosmicki's ("Getaround Defendants") demurrer to Plaintiff Geoffrey Shmigelsky's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action for fraudulent concealment and constructive fraud, sustained with 30 days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for aiding and abetting insider trading, and overruled as to the fifth cause of action for securities fraud. The sixth cause of action for insider trading is brought only against the Purchaser Defendants, so the Getaround Defendants do not have standing to demur to that claim. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed in open court.
